name: Black Sites
description: Black sites, in the context of global conspiracy theories and alternative history, refer to clandestine government facilities allegedly used for covert operations, often involving illegal or unethical activities. These activities purportedly include torture, illegal experimentation, advanced technology research (potentially involving UFOs or extraterrestrial contact), and detention of individuals without due process. The concept gained prominence following reports and investigations into the CIA's use of secret detention facilities after the 9/11 attacks. Proponents of broader conspiracy theories extend this concept, claiming the existence of numerous undeclared black sites globally, operated by various governments and shadowy organizations, engaged in activities hidden from public scrutiny. Evidence cited often includes leaked documents (sometimes of questionable authenticity), whistleblower testimonies (which are often disputed), and circumstantial evidence interpreted through a lens of suspicion. Mainstream perspectives acknowledge the existence of declared and undeclared military and intelligence facilities, but generally deny the existence of the more extreme claims associated with black site conspiracy theories. Academic and journalistic investigations have confirmed the use of secret detention facilities by the US government, but the scope and nature of these facilities, and the activities conducted within them, remain subjects of ongoing debate. The claims of advanced technology research and extraterrestrial involvement are largely dismissed by mainstream science and government officials as unsubstantiated.
subtitle: Clandestine facilities and covert operations.

claims.claim: Governments operate secret facilities for illegal activities.
claims.evidence: Leaked documents, whistleblower testimonies, circumstantial evidence.
claims.criticism: Documents often lack verification; whistleblower accounts can be biased; circumstantial evidence is open to interpretation.

claims.claim: Black sites are used for torture and illegal experimentation.
claims.evidence: Reports of prisoner abuse, allegations of unethical research practices.
claims.criticism: Reports of abuse are investigated and prosecuted; lack of concrete evidence for widespread experimentation.

claims.claim: Advanced technology research, possibly involving UFOs, occurs at black sites.
claims.evidence: Unexplained phenomena near alleged sites, purported leaks about reverse engineering programs.
claims.criticism: Unexplained phenomena have alternative explanations; leaks are often unsubstantiated or hoaxes.

keyFigures.name: Joseph Trento
keyFigures.role: Author focusing on intelligence community secrets.

keyFigures.name: Steven Aftergood
keyFigures.role: Director of the Project on Government Secrecy at the Federation of American Scientists.

timeline.event: Emergence of Cold War intelligence activities.
MKUltra and other controversial government programs.
Increased focus on CIA black sites and enhanced interrogation techniques.
